entities.name: VESICULAR-ARBUSCULAR MYCORRHIZA
entities.description: Fungi that form symbiotic relationships with plant roots, identified by spores and infective propagules

entities.name: EARTHWORM
entities.description: Soil-dwelling organisms that produce casts and can concentrate fungal spores

entities.name: EARTHWORM CASTS
entities.description: The waste material excreted by earthworms, which can concentrate VAM spores and propagules

entities.name: FIELD SOIL
entities.description: The surrounding soil environment used as a control or comparison for VAM spore and propagule counts

entities.name: SOIL MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The overall practice of managing soil through tillage and amendments

entities.name: ZERO TILLAGE WITH STRAW AMENDMENT
entities.description: A soil management treatment characterized by no tillage and the addition of straw

entities.name: DEEP TILLAGE WITHOUT AMENDMENT
entities.description: A soil management treatment characterized by deep tillage and bare soil

entities.name: ZERO TILLAGE WITH STYLOSANTHES HAMATA
entities.description: A soil management treatment characterized by no tillage and previous perennial cropping of Stylosanthes hamata

entities.name: STYLOSANTHES HAMATA
entities.description: A perennial legume used in a specific soil management treatment

entities.name: ALFISOL
entities.description: A specific type of soil characterized by its mineral and organic properties, found in the semi-arid tropics

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: A climatic region characterized by low and seasonal rainfall

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the study on Alfisol soil was conducted

entities.name: SPORE COUNTS
entities.description: The measured number of VAM spores in a given sample of soil or casts

entities.name: MOST PROBABLE NUMBER OF INFECTIVE PROPAGULES
entities.description: A statistical estimate of the number of viable and infective VAM propagules in a sample

relationships.source: MOST PROBABLE NUMBER OF INFECTIVE PROPAGULES

relationships.description: resulted in higher spore counts in casts
